SHEET METAL AND WOODEN DEBRIS ON THE GROUND. TIME OF DAMAGE ESTIMATED BY RADAR.

THE TORNADO BEGAN ABOUT 10 MILES SOUTHWEST OF KONAWA NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF HIGHWAY 3W AND CR 1450. A BARN WAS DESTROYED AND A COUPLE OF OTHER FAMR BUILDINGS WERE DAMAGED. THE CIRCULATION MOVED INTO AN AREA INACCESSIBLE BY ROADS SO NO ADDITIONAL DAMAGE WAS FOUND. THIS EVENT IS STILL BEING INVESTIGATED AND ADDITIONAL DETAILS WILL BE AVAILABLE LATER. PRELIMINARY RATING EF-1.

THE TORNADO DEVELOPED ABOUT 3 MILES NORTHEAST OF KONAWA FROM THE SAME CIRCULATION THAT PRODUCED THE TORNADO THAT OCCURRED EARLIER SOUTHWEST OF KONAWA. SEVERAL WOODEN POWER POLES WERE SNAPPED, AND TREES WERE DAMAGED ALONG EW 1400 RD ABOUT A MILE WEST OF HIGHWAY 99. THE REMAINDER OF THE PATH WAS NOT SURVED DUE TO IT BEING PARTIALLY INACCESSIBLE BY ROADS. PRELIMINARY RATING EF-1.
